NKJV.1: In the meantime, when an innumerable multitude of people had gathered together, so that they trampled one another, He began to say to His disciples first of all, “Beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy.
NKJV.2: For there is nothing covered that will not be revealed, nor hidden that will not be known.
NKJV.3: Therefore whatever you have spoken in the dark will be heard in the light, and what you have spoken in the ear in inner rooms will be proclaimed on the housetops.
NKJV.4: “And I say to you,  My friends, do not be afraid of those who kill the body, and after that have no more that they can do.
NKJV.5: But I will show you whom you should fear: Fear Him who, after He has killed, has power to cast into hell; yes, I say to you,  fear Him!
NKJV.6: “Are not five sparrows sold for two copper coins? And  not one of them is forgotten before God.
NKJV.7: But the very hairs of your head are all numbered. Do not fear therefore; you are of more value than many sparrows.
NKJV.8: “Also I say to you, whoever confesses Me  before men, him the Son of Man also will confess before the angels of God.
NKJV.9: But he who  denies Me before men will be denied before the angels of God.
NKJV.10: “And  anyone who speaks a word against the Son of Man, it will be forgiven him; but to him who blasphemes against the Holy Spirit, it will not be forgiven.
NKJV.11: “Now when they bring you to the synagogues and magistrates and authorities, do not worry about how or what you should answer, or what you should say.
NKJV.12: For the Holy Spirit will  teach you in that very hour what you ought to say.”
NKJV.13: Then one from the crowd said to Him, “Teacher, tell my brother to divide the inheritance with me.”
NKJV.14: But He said to him, “Man, who made Me a judge or an arbitrator over you?”
NKJV.15: And He said to them, “Take heed and beware of covetousness, for one’s life does not consist in the abundance of the things he possesses.”
NKJV.16: Then He spoke a parable to them, saying: “The ground of a certain rich man yielded plentifully.
NKJV.17: And he thought within himself, saying, ‘What shall I do, since I have no room to store my crops?’
NKJV.18: So he said, ‘I will do this: I will pull down my barns and build greater, and there I will store all my crops and my goods.
NKJV.19: And I will say to my soul, “Soul, you have many goods laid up for many years; take your ease; eat, drink, and be merry.” ’
NKJV.20: But God said to him, ‘Fool! This night  your soul will be required of you;  then whose will those things be which you have provided?’
NKJV.21: “So is he who lays up treasure for himself, and is not rich toward God.”
NKJV.22: Then He said to His disciples, “Therefore I say to you,  do not worry about your life, what you will eat; nor about the body, what you will put on.
NKJV.23: Life is more than food, and the body is more than clothing.
NKJV.24: Consider the ravens, for they neither sow nor reap, which have neither storehouse nor barn; and  God feeds them. Of how much more value are you than the birds?
NKJV.25: And which of you by worrying can add one cubit to his stature?
NKJV.26: If you then are not able to do the least, why are you anxious for the rest?
NKJV.27: Consider the lilies, how they grow: they neither toil nor spin; and yet I say to you, even  Solomon in all his glory was not arrayed like one of these.
NKJV.28: If then God so clothes the grass, which today is in the field and tomorrow is thrown into the oven, how much more will He clothe you, O you of little faith?
NKJV.29: “And do not seek what you should eat or what you should drink, nor have an anxious mind.
NKJV.30: For all these things the nations of the world seek after, and your Father  knows that you need these things.
NKJV.31: But seek the kingdom of God, and all these things shall be added to you.
NKJV.32: “Do not fear, little flock, for  it is your Father’s good pleasure to give you the kingdom.
NKJV.33: Sell what you have and give  alms;  provide yourselves money bags which do not grow old, a treasure in the heavens that does not fail, where no thief approaches nor moth destroys.
NKJV.34: For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
NKJV.35: “Let your waist be girded and your lamps burning;
NKJV.36: and you yourselves be like men who wait for their master, when he will return from the wedding, that when he comes and knocks they may open to him immediately.
NKJV.37: Blessed are those servants whom the master, when he comes, will find watching. Assuredly, I say to you that he will gird himself and have them sit down to eat, and will come and serve them.
NKJV.38: And if he should come in the second watch, or come in the third watch, and find them so, blessed are those servants.
NKJV.39: But know this, that if the master of the house had known what hour the thief would come, he would have watched and not allowed his house to be broken into.
NKJV.40: Therefore you also be ready, for the Son of Man is coming at an hour you do not expect.”
NKJV.41: Then Peter said to Him, “Lord, do You speak this parable only to us, or to all people?”
NKJV.42: And the Lord said, “Who then is that faithful and wise steward, whom his master will make ruler over his household, to give them their portion of food in due season?
NKJV.43: Blessed is that servant whom his master will find so doing when he comes.
NKJV.44: Truly, I say to you that he will make him ruler over all that he has.
NKJV.45: But if that servant says in his heart, ‘My master is delaying his coming,’ and begins to beat the male and female servants, and to eat and drink and be drunk,
NKJV.46: the master of that servant will come on a day when he is not looking for him, and at an hour when he is not aware, and will cut him in two and appoint him his portion with the unbelievers.
NKJV.47: And that servant who knew his master’s will, and did not prepare himself or do according to his will, shall be beaten with many stripes.
NKJV.48: But he who did not know, yet committed things deserving of stripes, shall be beaten with few. For everyone to whom much is given, from him much will be required; and to whom much has been committed, of him they will ask the more.
NKJV.49: “I came to send fire on the earth, and how I wish it were already kindled!
NKJV.50: But  I have a baptism to be baptized with, and how distressed I am till it is  accomplished!
NKJV.51: Do you suppose that I came to give peace on earth? I tell you, not at all, but rather division.
NKJV.52: For from now on five in one house will be divided: three against two, and two against three.
NKJV.53: Father will be divided against son and son against father, mother against daughter and daughter against mother, mother-in-law against her daughter-in-law and daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law.”
NKJV.54: Then He also said to the multitudes, “Whenever you see a cloud rising out of the west, immediately you say, ‘A shower is coming’; and so it is.
NKJV.55: And when you see the south wind blow, you say, ‘There will be hot weather’; and there is.

NKJV.57: “Yes, and why, even of yourselves, do you not judge what is right?
NKJV.58: When you go with your adversary to the magistrate, make every effort  along the way to settle with him, lest he drag you to the judge, the judge deliver you to the officer, and the officer throw you into prison.

ESV.1: In the meantime, when so many thousands of the people had gathered together that they were trampling one another, he began to say to his disciples first,  “Beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy.
ESV.2: Nothing is covered up that will not be revealed, or hidden that will not be known.
ESV.3: Therefore whatever you have said in the dark shall be heard in the light, and what you have whispered in private rooms shall be proclaimed on the housetops.
ESV.4: “I tell you, my friends, do not fear those who kill the body, and after that have nothing more that they can do.
ESV.5: But I will warn you whom to fear: fear him who, after he has killed, has authority to cast into hell.  Yes, I tell you, fear him!
ESV.6: Are not five sparrows sold for two pennies?  And not one of them is forgotten before God.
ESV.7: Why, even the hairs of your head are all numbered. Fear not; you are of more value than many sparrows.
ESV.8: “And I tell you, everyone who acknowledges me before men, the Son of Man also will acknowledge before the angels of God,
ESV.9: but the one who denies me before men will be denied before the angels of God.
ESV.10: And everyone who speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven, but the one who blasphemes against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven.
ESV.11: And when they bring you before the synagogues and the rulers and the authorities, do not be anxious about how you should defend yourself or what you should say,
ESV.12: for the Holy Spirit will teach you in that very hour what you ought to say.”
ESV.13: Someone in the crowd said to him, “Teacher, tell my brother to divide the inheritance with me.”
ESV.14: But he said to him,  “Man, who made me a judge or arbitrator over you?”
ESV.15: And he said to them,  “Take care, and be on your guard against all covetousness, for one's life does not consist in the abundance of his possessions.”
ESV.16: And he told them a parable, saying,  “The land of a rich man produced plentifully,
ESV.17: and he thought to himself, ‘What shall I do, for I have nowhere to store my crops?’
ESV.18: And he said, ‘I will do this: I will tear down my barns and build larger ones, and there I will store all my grain and my goods.
ESV.19: And I will say to my soul, “Soul, you have ample goods laid up for many years; relax, eat, drink, be merry.”’
ESV.20: But God said to him, ‘Fool! This night your soul is required of you, and the things you have prepared, whose will they be?’
ESV.21: So is the one who lays up treasure for himself and is not rich toward God.”
ESV.22: And he said to his disciples,  “Therefore I tell you, do not be anxious about your life, what you will eat, nor about your body, what you will put on.
ESV.23: For life is more than food, and the body more than clothing.
ESV.24: Consider the ravens: they neither sow nor reap, they have neither storehouse nor barn, and yet God feeds them. Of how much more value are you than the birds!
ESV.25: And which of you by being anxious can add a single hour to his span of life?
ESV.26: If then you are not able to do as small a thing as that, why are you anxious about the rest?
ESV.27: Consider the lilies, how they grow: they neither toil nor spin,  yet I tell you, even Solomon in all his glory was not arrayed like one of these.
ESV.28: But if God so clothes the grass, which is alive in the field today, and tomorrow is thrown into the oven, how much more will he clothe you, O you of little faith!
ESV.29: And do not seek what you are to eat and what you are to drink, nor be worried.
ESV.30: For all the nations of the world seek after these things, and your Father knows that you need them.
ESV.31: Instead, seek his  kingdom, and these things will be added to you.
ESV.32: “Fear not, little flock, for it is your Father's good pleasure to give you the kingdom.
ESV.33: Sell your possessions, and give to the needy. Provide yourselves with moneybags that do not grow old, with a treasure in the heavens that does not fail, where no thief approaches and no moth destroys.
ESV.34: For where your treasure is, there will your heart be also.
ESV.35: “Stay dressed for action  and keep your lamps burning,
ESV.36: and be like men who are waiting for their master to come home from the wedding feast, so that they may open the door to him at once when he comes and knocks.
ESV.37: Blessed are those servants  whom the master finds awake when he comes. Truly, I say to you, he will dress himself for service and have them recline at table, and he will come and serve them.
ESV.38: If he comes in the second watch, or in the third, and finds them awake, blessed are those servants!
ESV.39: But know this, that if the master of the house had known at what hour the thief was coming, he  would not have left his house to be broken into.
ESV.40: You also must be ready, for the Son of Man is coming at an hour you do not expect.”
ESV.41: Peter said, “Lord, are you telling this parable for us or for all?”
ESV.42: And the Lord said, “Who then is the faithful and wise manager, whom his master will set over his household, to give them their portion of food at the proper time?
ESV.43: Blessed is that servant  whom his master will find so doing when he comes.
ESV.44: Truly, I say to you, he will set him over all his possessions.
ESV.45: But if that servant says to himself, ‘My master is delayed in coming,’ and begins to beat the male and female servants, and to eat and drink and get drunk,
ESV.46: the master of that servant will come on a day when he does not expect him and at an hour he does not know, and will cut him in pieces and put him with the unfaithful.
ESV.47: And that servant who knew his master's will but did not get ready or act according to his will, will receive a severe beating.
ESV.48: But the one who did not know, and did what deserved a beating, will receive a light beating. Everyone to whom much was given, of him much will be required, and from him to whom they entrusted much, they will demand the more.
ESV.49: “I came to cast fire on the earth, and would that it were already kindled!
ESV.50: I have a baptism to be baptized with, and how great is my distress until it is accomplished!
ESV.51: Do you think that I have come to give peace on earth? No, I tell you, but rather division.
ESV.52: For from now on in one house there will be five divided, three against two and two against three.
ESV.53: They will be divided, father against son and son against father, mother against daughter and daughter against mother, mother-in-law against her daughter-in-law and daughter-in-law against mother-in-law.”
ESV.54: He also said to the crowds,  “When you see a cloud rising in the west, you say at once, ‘A shower is coming.’ And so it happens.
ESV.55: And when you see the south wind blowing, you say, ‘There will be scorching heat,’ and it happens.
ESV.56: You hypocrites! You know how to interpret the appearance of earth and sky, but why do you not know how to interpret the present time?
ESV.57: “And why do you not judge for yourselves what is right?
ESV.58: As you go with your accuser before the magistrate, make an effort to settle with him on the way, lest he drag you to the judge, and the judge hand you over to the officer, and the officer put you in prison.
ESV.59: I tell you, you will never get out until you have paid the very last penny.”

NASB.21: 'So is the man who stores up treasure for himself, and is not rich toward God.'

NASB.23: 'For life is more than food, and the body more than clothing.
NASB.24: 'Consider the ravens, for they neither sow nor reap; they have no storeroom nor barn, and [yet] God feeds them; how much more valuable you are than the birds!
NASB.25: 'And which of you by worrying can add a [single] hour to his life's span?
NASB.26: 'If then you cannot do even a very little thing, why do you worry about other matters?
NASB.27: 'Consider the lilies, how they grow: they neither toil nor spin; but I tell you, not even Solomon in all his glory clothed himself like one of these.
NASB.28: 'But if God so clothes the grass in the field, which is [alive] today and tomorrow is thrown into the furnace, how much more [will He clothe] you? You men of little faith!
NASB.29: 'And do not seek what you will eat and what you will drink, and do not keep worrying.
NASB.30: 'For all these things the nations of the world eagerly seek; but your Father knows that you need these things.
NASB.31: 'But seek His kingdom, and these things will be added to you.
NASB.32: 'Do not be afraid, little flock, for your Father has chosen gladly to give you the kingdom.
NASB.33: 'Sell your possessions and give to charity; make yourselves money belts which do not wear out, an unfailing treasure in heaven, where no thief comes near nor moth destroys.

NASB.59: 'I say to you, you will not get out of there until you have paid the very last cent.'

NLT.1: Meanwhile, the crowds grew until thousands were milling about and stepping on each other. Jesus turned first to his disciples and warned them, “Beware of the yeast of the Pharisees — their hypocrisy.
NLT.2: The time is coming when everything that is covered up will be revealed, and all that is secret will be made known to all.
NLT.3: Whatever you have said in the dark will be heard in the light, and what you have whispered behind closed doors will be shouted from the housetops for all to hear!
NLT.4: “Dear friends, don’t be afraid of those who want to kill your body; they cannot do any more to you after that.
NLT.5: But I’ll tell you whom to fear. Fear God, who has the power to kill you and then throw you into hell. Yes, he’s the one to fear.
NLT.6: “What is the price of five sparrows — two copper coins ? Yet God does not forget a single one of them.
NLT.7: And the very hairs on your head are all numbered. So don’t be afraid; you are more valuable to God than a whole flock of sparrows.
NLT.8: “I tell you the truth, everyone who acknowledges me publicly here on earth, the Son of Man will also acknowledge in the presence of God’s angels.
NLT.9: But anyone who denies me here on earth will be denied before God’s angels.
NLT.10: Anyone who speaks against the Son of Man can be forgiven, but anyone who blasphemes the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven.
NLT.11: “And when you are brought to trial in the synagogues and before rulers and authorities, don’t worry about how to defend yourself or what to say,
NLT.12: for the Holy Spirit will teach you at that time what needs to be said.”
NLT.13: Then someone called from the crowd, “Teacher, please tell my brother to divide our father’s estate with me.”
NLT.14: Jesus replied, “Friend, who made me a judge over you to decide such things as that?”
NLT.15: Then he said, “Beware! Guard against every kind of greed. Life is not measured by how much you own.”
NLT.16: Then he told them a story: “A rich man had a fertile farm that produced fine crops.
NLT.17: He said to himself, ‘What should I do? I don’t have room for all my crops.’
NLT.18: Then he said, ‘I know! I’ll tear down my barns and build bigger ones. Then I’ll have room enough to store all my wheat and other goods.
NLT.19: And I’ll sit back and say to myself, “My friend, you have enough stored away for years to come. Now take it easy! Eat, drink, and be merry!”’
NLT.20: “But God said to him, ‘You fool! You will die this very night. Then who will get everything you worked for?’
NLT.21: “Yes, a person is a fool to store up earthly wealth but not have a rich relationship with God.”
NLT.22: Then, turning to his disciples, Jesus said, “That is why I tell you not to worry about everyday life — whether you have enough food to eat or enough clothes to wear.
NLT.23: For life is more than food, and your body more than clothing.
NLT.24: Look at the ravens. They don’t plant or harvest or store food in barns, for God feeds them. And you are far more valuable to him than any birds!
NLT.25: Can all your worries add a single moment to your life?
NLT.26: And if worry can’t accomplish a little thing like that, what’s the use of worrying over bigger things?
NLT.27: “Look at the lilies and how they grow. They don’t work or make their clothing, yet Solomon in all his glory was not dressed as beautifully as they are.
NLT.28: And if God cares so wonderfully for flowers that are here today and thrown into the fire tomorrow, he will certainly care for you. Why do you have so little faith?
NLT.29: “And don’t be concerned about what to eat and what to drink. Don’t worry about such things.
NLT.30: These things dominate the thoughts of unbelievers all over the world, but your Father already knows your needs.
NLT.31: Seek the Kingdom of God above all else, and he will give you everything you need.
NLT.32: “So don’t be afraid, little flock. For it gives your Father great happiness to give you the Kingdom.
NLT.33: “Sell your possessions and give to those in need. This will store up treasure for you in heaven! And the purses of heaven never get old or develop holes. Your treasure will be safe; no thief can steal it and no moth can destroy it.
NLT.34: Wherever your treasure is, there the desires of your heart will also be.
NLT.35: “Be dressed for service and keep your lamps burning,
NLT.36: as though you were waiting for your master to return from the wedding feast. Then you will be ready to open the door and let him in the moment he arrives and knocks.
NLT.37: The servants who are ready and waiting for his return will be rewarded. I tell you the truth, he himself will seat them, put on an apron, and serve them as they sit and eat!
NLT.38: He may come in the middle of the night or just before dawn. But whenever he comes, he will reward the servants who are ready.
NLT.39: “Understand this: If a homeowner knew exactly when a burglar was coming, he would not permit his house to be broken into.
NLT.40: You also must be ready all the time, for the Son of Man will come when least expected.”
NLT.41: Peter asked, “Lord, is that illustration just for us or for everyone?”
NLT.42: And the Lord replied, “A faithful, sensible servant is one to whom the master can give the responsibility of managing his other household servants and feeding them.
NLT.43: If the master returns and finds that the servant has done a good job, there will be a reward.
NLT.44: I tell you the truth, the master will put that servant in charge of all he owns.
NLT.45: But what if the servant thinks, ‘My master won’t be back for a while,’ and he begins beating the other servants, partying, and getting drunk?
NLT.46: The master will return unannounced and unexpected, and he will cut the servant in pieces and banish him with the unfaithful.
NLT.47: “And a servant who knows what the master wants, but isn’t prepared and doesn’t carry out those instructions, will be severely punished.
NLT.48: But someone who does not know, and then does something wrong, will be punished only lightly. When someone has been given much, much will be required in return; and when someone has been entrusted with much, even more will be required.
NLT.49: “I have come to set the world on fire, and I wish it were already burning!
NLT.50: I have a terrible baptism of suffering ahead of me, and I am under a heavy burden until it is accomplished.
NLT.51: Do you think I have come to bring peace to the earth? No, I have come to divide people against each other!
NLT.52: From now on families will be split apart, three in favor of me, and two against — or two in favor and three against.
NLT.53:
‘Father will be divided against son
and son against father;
mother against daughter
and daughter against mother;
and mother-in-law against daughter-in-law
and daughter-in-law against mother-in-law.’ ”
NLT.54: Then Jesus turned to the crowd and said, “When you see clouds beginning to form in the west, you say, ‘Here comes a shower.’ And you are right.
NLT.55: When the south wind blows, you say, ‘Today will be a scorcher.’ And it is.
NLT.56: You fools! You know how to interpret the weather signs of the earth and sky, but you don’t know how to interpret the present times.
NLT.57: “Why can’t you decide for yourselves what is right?
NLT.58: When you are on the way to court with your accuser, try to settle the matter before you get there. Otherwise, your accuser may drag you before the judge, who will hand you over to an officer, who will throw you into prison.
NLT.59: And if that happens, you won’t be free again until you have paid the very last penny. ”

WEB.1: Meanwhile, when a multitude of many thousands had gathered together, so much so that they trampled on each other, he began to tell his disciples first of all, “Beware of the yeast of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy.
WEB.2: But there is nothing covered up, that will not be revealed, nor hidden, that will not be known.
WEB.3: Therefore whatever you have said in the darkness will be heard in the light. What you have spoken in the ear in the inner rooms will be proclaimed on the housetops.
WEB.4: “I tell you, my friends, don’t be afraid of those who kill the body, and after that have no more that they can do.
WEB.5: But I will warn you whom you should fear. Fear him, who after he has killed, has power to cast into Gehenna.  Yes, I tell you, fear him.
WEB.6: “Aren’t five sparrows sold for two assaria coins  ? Not one of them is forgotten by God.
WEB.7: But the very hairs of your head are all counted. Therefore don’t be afraid. You are of more value than many sparrows.
WEB.8: “I tell you, everyone who confesses me before men, him will the Son of Man also confess before the angels of God;
WEB.9: but he who denies me in the presence of men will be denied in the presence of the angels of God.
WEB.10: Everyone who speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven, but those who blaspheme against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven.
WEB.11: When they bring you before the synagogues, the rulers, and the authorities, don’t be anxious how or what you will answer, or what you will say;
WEB.12: for the Holy Spirit will teach you in that same hour what you must say.”
WEB.13: One of the multitude said to him, “Teacher, tell my brother to divide the inheritance with me.”
WEB.14: But he said to him, “Man, who made me a judge or an arbitrator over you?”
WEB.15: He said to them, “Beware! Keep yourselves from covetousness, for a man’s life doesn’t consist of the abundance of the things which he possesses.”
WEB.16: He spoke a parable to them, saying, “The ground of a certain rich man produced abundantly.
WEB.17: He reasoned within himself, saying, ‘What will I do, because I don’t have room to store my crops?’
WEB.18: He said, ‘This is what I will do. I will pull down my barns, and build bigger ones, and there I will store all my grain and my goods.
WEB.19: I will tell my soul, “Soul, you have many goods laid up for many years. Take your ease, eat, drink, be merry.”’
WEB.20: “But God said to him, ‘You foolish one, tonight your soul is required of you. The things which you have prepared — whose will they be?’
WEB.21: So is he who lays up treasure for himself, and is not rich toward God.”
WEB.22: He said to his disciples, “Therefore I tell you, don’t be anxious for your life, what you will eat, nor yet for your body, what you will wear.
WEB.23: Life is more than food, and the body is more than clothing.
WEB.24: Consider the ravens: they don’t sow, they don’t reap, they have no warehouse or barn, and God feeds them. How much more valuable are you than birds!
WEB.25: Which of you by being anxious can add a cubit  to his height?
WEB.26: If then you aren’t able to do even the least things, why are you anxious about the rest?
WEB.27: Consider the lilies, how they grow. They don’t toil, neither do they spin; yet I tell you, even Solomon in all his glory was not arrayed like one of these.
WEB.28: But if this is how God clothes the grass in the field, which today exists, and tomorrow is cast into the oven, how much more will he clothe you, O you of little faith?
WEB.29: Don’t seek what you will eat or what you will drink; neither be anxious.
WEB.30: For the nations of the world seek after all of these things, but your Father knows that you need these things.
WEB.31: But seek God’s Kingdom, and all these things will be added to you.
WEB.32: Don’t be afraid, little flock, for it is your Father’s good pleasure to give you the Kingdom.
WEB.33: Sell that which you have, and give gifts to the needy. Make for yourselves purses which don’t grow old, a treasure in the heavens that doesn’t fail, where no thief approaches, neither moth destroys.
WEB.34: For where your treasure is, there will your heart be also.
WEB.35: “Let your waist be dressed and your lamps burning.
WEB.36: Be like men watching for their lord, when he returns from the marriage feast; that, when he comes and knocks, they may immediately open to him.
WEB.37: Blessed are those servants, whom the lord will find watching when he comes. Most certainly I tell you, that he will dress himself, and make them recline, and will come and serve them.
WEB.38: They will be blessed if he comes in the second or third watch, and finds them so.
WEB.39: But know this, that if the master of the house had known in what hour the thief was coming, he would have watched, and not allowed his house to be broken into.
WEB.40: Therefore be ready also, for the Son of Man is coming in an hour that you don’t expect him.”
WEB.41: Peter said to him, “Lord, are you telling this parable to us, or to everybody?”
WEB.42: The Lord said, “Who then is the faithful and wise steward, whom his lord will set over his household, to give them their portion of food at the right times?
WEB.43: Blessed is that servant whom his lord will find doing so when he comes.
WEB.44: Truly I tell you, that he will set him over all that he has.
WEB.45: But if that servant says in his heart, ‘My lord delays his coming,’ and begins to beat the menservants and the maidservants, and to eat and drink, and to be drunken,
WEB.46: then the lord of that servant will come in a day when he isn’t expecting him, and in an hour that he doesn’t know, and will cut him in two, and place his portion with the unfaithful.
WEB.47: That servant, who knew his lord’s will, and didn’t prepare, nor do what he wanted, will be beaten with many stripes,
WEB.48: but he who didn’t know, and did things worthy of stripes, will be beaten with few stripes. To whomever much is given, of him will much be required; and to whom much was entrusted, of him more will be asked.
WEB.49: “I came to throw fire on the earth. I wish it were already kindled.
WEB.50: But I have a baptism to be baptized with, and how distressed I am until it is accomplished!
WEB.51: Do you think that I have come to give peace in the earth? I tell you, no, but rather division.
WEB.52: For from now on, there will be five in one house divided, three against two, and two against three.
WEB.53: They will be divided, father against son, and son against father; mother against daughter, and daughter against her mother; mother-in-law against her daughter-in-law, and daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law.”
WEB.54: He said to the multitudes also, “When you see a cloud rising from the west, immediately you say, ‘A shower is coming,’ and so it happens.
WEB.55: When a south wind blows, you say, ‘There will be a scorching heat,’ and it happens.
WEB.56: You hypocrites! You know how to interpret the appearance of the earth and the sky, but how is it that you don’t interpret this time?
WEB.57: Why don’t you judge for yourselves what is right?
WEB.58: For when you are going with your adversary before the magistrate, try diligently on the way to be released from him, lest perhaps he drag you to the judge, and the judge deliver you to the officer, and the officer throw you into prison.
WEB.59: I tell you, you will by no means get out of there, until you have paid the very last penny. ”

AMP.1: In the meantime, after so many thousands of the people had gathered that they were stepping on one another, Jesus began speaking first of all to His disciples, "Be continually on your guard against the leaven of the Pharisees [that is, their pervasive, corrupting influence and teaching], which is hypocrisy [producing self-righteousness].
AMP.2: But there is nothing [so carefully] concealed that it will not be revealed, nor so hidden that it will not be made known.
AMP.3: For that reason, whatever you have said in the dark will be heard in the light, and what you have whispered behind closed doors will be proclaimed on the housetops.
AMP.4: "I say to you, My friends, do not be afraid of those who kill the body and after that have nothing more that they can do.
AMP.5: But I will point out to you whom you should fear: fear the One who, after He has killed, has authority and power to hurl [you] into hell; yes, I say to you, [stand in great awe of God and] fear Him!
AMP.6: Are not five sparrows sold for two copper coins? Yet not one of them has [ever] been forgotten in the presence of God.
AMP.7: Indeed the very hairs of your head are all numbered. Do not be afraid; you are far more valuable than many sparrows.
AMP.8: "I say to you, whoever declares openly and confesses Me before men [speaking freely of Me as his Lord], the Son of Man also will declare openly and confess him [as one of His own] before the angels of God.
AMP.9: But he who denies Me before men will be denied in the presence of the angels of God.
AMP.10: And everyone who speaks a word against the Son of Man, it will be forgiven him; but he who blasphemes against the Holy Spirit [that is, whoever intentionally discredits the Holy Spirit by attributing the authenticating miracles done by Me to Satan], it will not be forgiven him [for him there is no forgiveness].
AMP.11: When they bring you before the synagogues and the magistrates and the authorities, do not be worried about how you are to defend yourselves or what you are to say;
AMP.12: for the Holy Spirit will teach you in that very hour what you ought to say."
AMP.13: Someone from the crowd said to Him, "Teacher, tell my brother to divide the family inheritance with me."
AMP.14: But He said to him, "Man, who appointed Me a judge or an arbitrator over [the two of] you?"
AMP.15: Then He said to them, "Watch out and guard yourselves against every form of greed; for not even when one has an overflowing abundance does his life consist of nor is it derived from his possessions."
AMP.16: Then He told them a parable, saying, "There was a rich man whose land was very fertile and productive.
AMP.17: And he began thinking to himself, 'What shall I do, since I have no place [large enough in which] to store my crops?'
AMP.18: Then he said, 'This is what I will do: I will tear down my storehouses and build larger ones, and I will store all my grain and my goods there.
AMP.19: And I will say to my soul, "Soul, you have many good things stored up, [enough] for many years; rest and relax, eat, drink and be merry (celebrate continually)."'
AMP.20: But God said to him, 'You fool! This very night your soul is required of you; and now who will own all the things you have prepared?'
AMP.21: So it is for the one who continues to store up and hoard possessions for himself, and is not rich [in his relationship] toward God."
AMP.22: Jesus said to His disciples, "For this reason I tell you, do not worry about your life, as to what you will eat; or about your body, as to what you will wear.
AMP.23: For life is more than food, and the body more than clothes.
AMP.24: Consider the ravens, for they neither sow [seed] nor reap [the crop]; they have no storehouse or barn, and yet God feeds them. How much more valuable are you than the birds!

AMP.28: But if this is how God clothes the grass which is in the field today and tomorrow is thrown into the furnace, how much more will He clothe you? You of little faith!

AMP.30: For all the [pagan] nations of the world greedily seek these things; and your [heavenly] Father [already] knows that you need them.
AMP.31: But [strive for and actively] seek His kingdom, and these things will be given to you as well.
AMP.32: Do not be afraid and anxious, little flock, for it is your Father's good pleasure to give you the kingdom.

AMP.34: For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
AMP.35: "Be dressed and ready for active service, and keep your lamps continuously burning.
AMP.36: Be like men who are waiting for their master when he returns from the wedding feast, so that when he comes and knocks they may immediately open the door for him.
AMP.37: Blessed (happy, prosperous, to be admired) are those servants whom the master finds awake and watching when he arrives. I assure you and most solemnly say to you, he will prepare himself to serve, and will have them recline at the table, and will come and wait on them.
AMP.38: Whether he comes in the second watch (before midnight), or even in the third (after midnight), and finds them so [prepared and ready], blessed are those servants.
AMP.39: "But be sure of this, that if the head of the house had known at what time the thief was coming, he [would have been awake and alert, and] would not have allowed his house to be broken into.
AMP.40: You too, be continually ready; because the Son of Man is coming at an hour that you do not expect."
AMP.41: Peter said, "Lord, are You addressing this parable to us [disciples], or to everyone else as well?"
AMP.42: The Lord said, "Who then is the faithful and wise steward [of the estate], whom his master will put in charge over his household, to give his servants their portion of food at the proper time?

AMP.44: I assure you and most solemnly say to you, he will put him in charge of all his possessions.
AMP.45: But if that servant says in his heart, 'My master is taking his time in coming,' and begins to beat the servants, both men and women, and to eat and drink and get drunk,

AMP.47: And that servant who knew his master's will, and yet did not get ready or act in accord with his will, will be beaten with many lashes [of the whip],

AMP.49: "I have come to cast fire (judgment) on the earth; and how I wish that it were already kindled!
AMP.50: I have a baptism [of great suffering] with which to be baptized, and how [greatly] I am distressed until it is accomplished!
AMP.51: Do you suppose that I came to grant peace on earth? No, I tell you, but rather division [between believers and unbelievers];
AMP.52: for from now on five in one household will be divided [over Me], three against two and two against three.
AMP.53: They will be divided, father against son and son against father, mother against daughter and daughter against mother, mother-in-law against daughter-in-law and daughter-in-law against mother-in-law."

AMP.55: And when [you see that] a south wind is blowing, you say, 'It will be a hot day,' and it happens.

AMP.57: "And why do you not even on your own initiative judge what is right?
AMP.58: For while you are going with your opponent [at law] to appear before a magistrate, on the way make an effort to settle, so that he does not drag you before the judge, and the judge does not [rule against you and] turn you over to the officer, and the officer does not throw you into prison.
AMP.59: I say to you, you [absolutely] will not get out of there until you have paid the very last cent."

MSG.1: By this time the crowd, unwieldy and stepping on each other's toes, numbered into the thousands. But Jesus' primary concern was his disciples. He said to them, "Watch yourselves carefully so you don't get contaminated with Pharisee yeast, Pharisee phoniness.
MSG.2: You can't keep your true self hidden forever; before long you'll be exposed. You can't hide behind a religious mask forever; sooner or later the mask will slip and your true face will be known.
MSG.3: You can't whisper one thing in private and preach the opposite in public; the day's coming when those whispers will be repeated all over town.
MSG.4: "I'm speaking to you as dear friends. Don't be bluffed into silence or insincerity by the threats of religious bullies. True, they can kill you, but then what can they do? There's nothing they can do to your soul, your core being.
MSG.5: Save your fear for God, who holds your entire life—body and soul—in his hands.
MSG.6: "What's the price of two or three pet canaries? Some loose change, right? But God never overlooks a single one.
MSG.7: And he pays even greater attention to you, down to the last detail—even numbering the hairs on your head! So don't be intimidated by all this bully talk. You're worth more than a million canaries.
MSG.8: "Stand up for me among the people you meet and the Son of Man will stand up for you before all God's angels.
MSG.9: But if you pretend you don't know me, do you think I'll defend you before God's angels?
MSG.10: "If you bad-mouth the Son of Man out of misunderstanding or ignorance, that can be overlooked. But if you're knowingly attacking God himself, taking aim at the Holy Spirit, that won't be overlooked.
MSG.11: "When they drag you into their meeting places, or into police courts and before judges, don't worry about defending yourselves—what you'll say or how you'll say it.
MSG.12: The right words will be there. The Holy Spirit will give you the right words when the time comes."
MSG.13: Someone out of the crowd said, "Teacher, order my brother to give me a fair share of the family inheritance."
MSG.14: He replied, "Mister, what makes you think it's any of my business to be a judge or mediator for you?"
MSG.15: peaking to the people, he went on, "STake care! Protect yourself against the least bit of greed. Life is not defined by what you have, even when you have a lot."
MSG.16: Then he told them this story: "The farm of a certain rich man produced a terrific crop.
MSG.17: He talked to himself: 'What can I do? My barn isn't big enough for this harvest.'
MSG.18: Then he said, 'Here's what I'll do: I'll tear down my barns and build bigger ones. Then I'll gather in all my grain and goods,
MSG.19: and I'll say to myself, Self, you've done well! You've got it made and can now retire. Take it easy and have the time of your life!'
MSG.20: "Just then God showed up and said, 'Fool! Tonight you die. And your barnful of goods—who gets it?'
MSG.21: "That's what happens when you fill your barn with Self and not with God."
MSG.22: He continued this subject with his disciples. "Don't fuss about what's on the table at mealtimes or if the clothes in your closet are in fashion.
MSG.23: There is far more to your inner life than the food you put in your stomach, more to your outer appearance than the clothes you hang on your body.
MSG.24: Look at the ravens, free and unfettered, not tied down to a job description, carefree in the care of God. And you count far more.
MSG.25: "Has anyone by fussing before the mirror ever gotten taller by so much as an inch?
MSG.26: If fussing can't even do that, why fuss at all?
MSG.27: Walk into the fields and look at the wildflowers. They don't fuss with their appearance—but have you ever seen color and design quite like it? The ten best-dressed men and women in the country look shabby alongside them.
MSG.28: If God gives such attention to the wildflowers, most of them never even seen, don't you think he'll attend to you, take pride in you, do his best for you?
MSG.29: "What I'm trying to do here is get you to relax, not be so preoccupied with getting so you can respond to God's giving.
MSG.30: People who don't know God and the way he works fuss over these things, but you know both God and how he works.
MSG.31: Steep yourself in God-reality, God-initiative, God-provisions. You'll find all your everyday human concerns will be met.
MSG.32: Don't be afraid of missing out. You're my dearest friends! The Father wants to give you the very kingdom itself.
MSG.33: "Be generous. Give to the poor. Get yourselves a bank that can't go bankrupt, a bank in heaven far from bankrobbers, safe from embezzlers, a bank you can bank on.
MSG.34: It's obvious, isn't it? The place where your treasure is, is the place you will most want to be, and end up being.
MSG.35: "Keep your shirts on; keep the lights on!
MSG.36: Be like house servants waiting for their master to come back from his honeymoon, awake and ready to open the door when he arrives and knocks.
MSG.37: Lucky the servants whom the master finds on watch! He'll put on an apron, sit them at the table, and serve them a meal, sharing his wedding feast with them.
MSG.38: It doesn't matter what time of the night he arrives; they're awake—and so blessed!
MSG.39: "You know that if the house owner had known what night the burglar was coming, he wouldn't have stayed out late and left the place unlocked.
MSG.40: So don't you be slovenly and careless. Just when you don't expect him, the Son of Man will show up."
MSG.41: Peter said, "Master, are you telling this story just for us? Or is it for everybody?"
MSG.42: The Master said, "Let me ask you: Who is the dependable manager, full of common sense, that the master puts in charge of his staff to feed them well and on time?
MSG.43: He is a blessed man if when the master shows up he's doing his job.
MSG.45: But if he says to himself, 'The master is certainly taking his time,' begins maltreating the servants and maids, throws parties for his friends, and gets drunk,
MSG.46: the master will walk in when he least expects it, give him the thrashing of his life, and put him back in the kitchen peeling potatoes.
MSG.47: "The servant who knows what his master wants and ignores it, or insolently does whatever he pleases, will be thoroughly thrashed.
MSG.48: But if he does a poor job through ignorance, he'll get off with a slap on the hand. Great gifts mean great responsibilities; greater gifts, greater responsibilities!
MSG.49: "I've come to start a fire on this earth—how I wish it were blazing right now!
MSG.50: I've come to change everything, turn everything rightside up—how I long for it to be finished!
MSG.51: Do you think I came to smooth things over and make everything nice? Not so. I've come to disrupt and confront!
MSG.52: From now on, when you find five in a house, it will be— Three against two, and two against three;
MSG.53: Father against son, and son against father; Mother against daughter, and daughter against mother; Mother-in-law against bride, and bride against mother-in-law."
MSG.54: Then he turned to the crowd: "When you see clouds coming in from the west, you say, 'Storm's coming'—and you're right.
MSG.55: And when the wind comes out of the south, you say, 'This'll be a hot one'—and you're right.
MSG.56: Frauds! You know how to tell a change in the weather, so don't tell me you can't tell a change in the season, the God-season we're in right now.
MSG.57: "You don't have to be a genius to understand these things. Just use your common sense,
MSG.58: the kind you'd use if, while being taken to court, you decided to settle up with your accuser on the way, knowing that if the case went to the judge you'd probably go to jail
MSG.59: and pay every last penny of the fine. That's the kind of decision I'm asking you to make."

NIV.1:
Warnings and Encouragements
Meanwhile, when a crowd of many thousands had gathered, so that they were trampling on one another, Jesus began to speak first to his disciples, saying: “Be on your guard against the yeast of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy.
NIV.2: There is nothing concealed that will not be disclosed, or hidden that will not be made known.
NIV.3: What you have said in the dark will be heard in the daylight, and what you have whispered in the ear in the inner rooms will be proclaimed from the roofs.
NIV.4: “I tell you, my friends, do not be afraid of those who kill the body and after that can do no more.
NIV.5: But I will show you whom you should fear: Fear him who, after the killing of the body, has power to throw you into hell. Yes, I tell you, fear him.
NIV.6: Are not five sparrows sold for two pennies? Yet not one of them is forgotten by God.
NIV.7: Indeed, the very hairs of your head are all numbered. Don’t be afraid; you are worth more than many sparrows.
NIV.8: “I tell you, whoever acknowledges me before men, the Son of Man will also acknowledge him before the angels of God.
NIV.9: But he who disowns me before men will be disowned before the angels of God.
NIV.10: And everyone who speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven, but anyone who blasphemes against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven.
NIV.11: “When you are brought before synagogues, rulers and authorities, do not worry about how you will defend yourselves or what you will say,
NIV.12: for the Holy Spirit will teach you at that time what you should say.”
NIV.13:
The Parable of the Rich Fool
Someone in the crowd said to him, “Teacher, tell my brother to divide the inheritance with me.”
NIV.14: Jesus replied, “Man, who appointed me a judge or an arbiter between you?”
NIV.15: Then he said to them, “Watch out! Be on your guard against all kinds of greed; a man’s life does not consist in the abundance of his possessions.”
NIV.16: And he told them this parable: “The ground of a certain rich man produced a good crop.
NIV.17: He thought to himself, ‘What shall I do? I have no place to store my crops.’
NIV.18: “Then he said, ‘This is what I’ll do. I will tear down my barns and build bigger ones, and there I will store all my grain and my goods.
NIV.19: And I’ll say to myself, “You have plenty of good things laid up for many years. Take life easy; eat, drink and be merry.”’
NIV.20: “But God said to him, ‘You fool! This very night your life will be demanded from you. Then who will get what you have prepared for yourself?’
NIV.21: “This is how it will be with anyone who stores up things for himself but is not rich toward God.”
NIV.22:
Do Not Worry
Then Jesus said to his disciples: “Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat; or about your body, what you will wear.
NIV.23: Life is more than food, and the body more than clothes.
NIV.24: Consider the ravens: They do not sow or reap, they have no storeroom or barn; yet God feeds them. And how much more valuable you are than birds!
NIV.25: Who of you by worrying can add a single hour to his life?
NIV.26: Since you cannot do this very little thing, why do you worry about the rest?
NIV.27: “Consider how the lilies grow. They do not labor or spin. Yet I tell you, not even Solomon in all his splendor was dressed like one of these.
NIV.28: If that is how God clothes the grass of the field, which is here today, and tomorrow is thrown into the fire, how much more will he clothe you, O you of little faith!
NIV.29: And do not set your heart on what you will eat or drink; do not worry about it.
NIV.30: For the pagan world runs after all such things, and your Father knows that you need them.
NIV.31: But seek his kingdom, and these things will be given to you as well.
NIV.32: “Do not be afraid, little flock, for your Father has been pleased to give you the kingdom.
NIV.33: Sell your possessions and give to the poor. Provide purses for yourselves that will not wear out, a treasure in heaven that will not be exhausted, where no thief comes near and no moth destroys.
NIV.34: For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
NIV.35:
Watchfulness
“Be dressed ready for service and keep your lamps burning,
NIV.36: like men waiting for their master to return from a wedding banquet, so that when he comes and knocks they can immediately open the door for him.
NIV.37: It will be good for those servants whose master finds them watching when he comes. I tell you the truth, he will dress himself to serve, will have them recline at the table and will come and wait on them.
NIV.38: It will be good for those servants whose master finds them ready, even if he comes in the second or third watch of the night.
NIV.39: But understand this: If the owner of the house had known at what hour the thief was coming, he would not have let his house be broken into.
NIV.40: You also must be ready, because the Son of Man will come at an hour when you do not expect him.”
NIV.41: Peter asked, “Lord, are you telling this parable to us, or to everyone?”
NIV.42: The Lord answered, “Who then is the faithful and wise manager, whom the master puts in charge of his servants to give them their food allowance at the proper time?
NIV.43: It will be good for that servant whom the master finds doing so when he returns.
NIV.44: I tell you the truth, he will put him in charge of all his possessions.
NIV.45: But suppose the servant says to himself, ‘My master is taking a long time in coming,’ and he then begins to beat the menservants and maidservants and to eat and drink and get drunk.
NIV.46: The master of that servant will come on a day when he does not expect him and at an hour he is not aware of. He will cut him to pieces and assign him a place with the unbelievers.
NIV.47: “That servant who knows his master’s will and does not get ready or does not do what his master wants will be beaten with many blows.
NIV.48: But the one who does not know and does things deserving punishment will be beaten with few blows. From everyone who has been given much, much will be demanded; and from the one who has been entrusted with much, much more will be asked.
NIV.49:
Not Peace but Division
“I have come to bring fire on the earth, and how I wish it were already kindled!
NIV.50: But I have a baptism to undergo, and how distressed I am until it is completed!
NIV.51: Do you think I came to bring peace on earth? No, I tell you, but division.
NIV.52: From now on there will be five in one family divided against each other, three against two and two against three.
NIV.53: They will be divided, father against son and son against father, mother against daughter and daughter against mother, mother-in-law against daughter-in-law and daughter-in-law against mother-in-law.”
NIV.54:
Interpreting the Times
He said to the crowd: “When you see a cloud rising in the west, immediately you say, ‘It’s going to rain,’ and it does.
NIV.55: And when the south wind blows, you say, ‘It’s going to be hot,’ and it is.
NIV.56: Hypocrites! You know how to interpret the appearance of the earth and the sky. How is it that you don’t know how to interpret this present time?
NIV.57: “Why don’t you judge for yourselves what is right?
NIV.58: As you are going with your adversary to the magistrate, try hard to be reconciled to him on the way, or he may drag you off to the judge, and the judge turn you over to the officer, and the officer throw you into prison.
NIV.59: I tell you, you will not get out until you have paid the last penny.”

NET.9: But the one who denies me before men will be denied before God’s angels.
NET.10: And everyone who speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven, but the person who blasphemes against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven.
NET.11: But when they bring you before the synagogues, the rulers, and the authorities, do not worry about how you should make your defense or what you should say,
NET.12: for the Holy Spirit will teach you at that moment what you must say.”
NET.13: Then someone from the crowd said to him, “Teacher, tell my brother to divide the inheritance with me.”
NET.14: But Jesus said to him, “Man, who made me a judge or arbitrator between you two?”
NET.15: Then he said to them, “Watch out and guard yourself from all types of greed, because one’s life does not consist in the abundance of his possessions.”
NET.16: He then told them a parable: “The land of a certain rich man produced an abundant crop,
NET.17: so he thought to himself, ‘What should I do, for I have nowhere to store my crops?’
NET.18: Then he said, ‘I will do this: I will tear down my barns and build bigger ones, and there I will store all my grain and my goods.
NET.19: And I will say to myself, “You have plenty of goods stored up for many years; relax, eat, drink, celebrate!”’
NET.20: But God said to him, ‘You fool! This very night your life will be demanded back from you, but who will get what you have prepared for yourself?’
NET.21: So it is with the one who stores up riches for himself, but is not rich toward God.”
NET.22: Then Jesus said to his disciples, “Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat, or about your body, what you will wear.
NET.23: For there is more to life than food, and more to the body than clothing.
NET.24: Consider the ravens: They do not sow or reap, they have no storeroom or barn, yet God feeds them. How much more valuable are you than the birds!
NET.25: And which of you by worrying can add an hour to his life?
NET.26: So if you cannot do such a very little thing as this, why do you worry about the rest?
NET.27: Consider how the flowers grow; they do not work or spin. Yet I tell you, not even Solomon in all his glory was clothed like one of these!
NET.28: And if this is how God clothes the wild grass, which is here today and tomorrow is tossed into the fire to heat the oven, how much more will he clothe you, you people of little faith!
NET.29: So do not be overly concerned about what you will eat and what you will drink, and do not worry about such things.
NET.30: For all the nations of the world pursue these things, and your Father knows that you need them.
NET.31: Instead, pursue his kingdom, and these things will be given to you as well.
NET.32: “Do not be afraid, little flock, for your Father is well pleased to give you the kingdom.
NET.33: Sell your possessions and give to the poor. Provide yourselves purses that do not wear out — a treasure in heaven that never decreases, where no thief approaches and no moth destroys.
NET.34: For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
NET.35: “Get dressed for service and keep your lamps burning;
NET.36: be like people waiting for their master to come back from the wedding celebration, so that when he comes and knocks they can immediately open the door for him.
NET.37: Blessed are those slaves whom their master finds alert when he returns! I tell you the truth, he will dress himself to serve, have them take their place at the table, and will come and wait on them!
NET.38: Even if he comes in the second or third watch of the night and finds them alert, blessed are those slaves!
NET.39: But understand this: If the owner of the house had known at what hour the thief was coming, he would not have let his house be broken into.
NET.40: You also must be ready, because the Son of Man will come at an hour when you do not expect him.”
NET.41: Then Peter said, “Lord, are you telling this parable for us or for everyone?”
NET.42: The Lord replied, “Who then is the faithful and wise manager, whom the master puts in charge of his household servants, to give them their allowance of food at the proper time?
NET.43: Blessed is that slave whom his master finds at work when he returns.
NET.44: I tell you the truth, the master will put him in charge of all his possessions.
NET.45: But if that slave should say to himself, ‘My master is delayed in returning,’ and he begins to beat the other slaves, both men and women, and to eat, drink, and get drunk,
NET.46: then the master of that slave will come on a day when he does not expect him and at an hour he does not foresee, and will cut him in two, and assign him a place with the unfaithful.
NET.47: That servant who knew his master’s will but did not get ready or do what his master asked will receive a severe beating.
NET.48: But the one who did not know his master’s will and did things worthy of punishment will receive a light beating. From everyone who has been given much, much will be required, and from the one who has been entrusted with much, even more will be asked.
NET.49: “I have come to bring fire on the earth — and how I wish it were already kindled!
NET.50: I have a baptism to undergo, and how distressed I am until it is finished!
NET.51: Do you think I have come to bring peace on earth? No, I tell you, but rather division!
NET.52: For from now on there will be five in one household divided, three against two and two against three.
NET.53: They will be divided, father against son and son against father, mother against daughter and daughter against mother, mother-in-law against her daughter-in-law and daughter-in-law against mother-in-law.”
NET.54: Jesus also said to the crowds, “When you see a cloud rising in the west, you say at once, ‘A rainstorm is coming,’ and it does.
NET.55: And when you see the south wind blowing, you say, ‘There will be scorching heat,’ and there is.
NET.56: You hypocrites! You know how to interpret the appearance of the earth and the sky, but how can you not know how to interpret the present time?
NET.57: “And why don’t you judge for yourselves what is right?
NET.58: As you are going with your accuser before the magistrate, make an effort to settle with him on the way, so that he will not drag you before the judge, and the judge hand you over to the officer, and the officer throw you into prison.
NET.59: I tell you, you will never get out of there until you have paid the very last cent!”
